Off Calendar Jun-26-2018 Text Ruling

Motion For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Patricia McCoy Smith, Esq., Tel. 510.273.8880, for defendant John Fields;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Plaintiff stipulates to Judge Pro Tem Melinda Derish.

Matter called.

There being no other appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted: Plaintiff's Motion For Protective Order is denied. Plaintiff failed to meet his burden to show good cause for the order sought. (Fairmont Ins. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (2000) Cal. 4th 245, 255.) Further, the preponderance of the evidence indicates plaintiff raised questions of mail service to negotiate other matters. Sanctions are awarded against plaintiff in the amount of $450. Order signed. (See order filed this date for full terms.)

Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT/HEK)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Admission
Off Calendar May-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Harold P. Smith, Esq. 510-273-8880 of Smith LLP for Defendant John Fields.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CARL WESCOTT's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Admission.

Plaintiff's Motion for Reconsideration of Order Denying Motion to Compel Further Responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to submit a declaration that identified the "new or different facts, circumstances or law." (CCP Section 1008).

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Interrogatories And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-16-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Plaintiff Carl Wescott's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff's Interrogatories And Imposing Monetary Sanctions as follows: DENIED in whole. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and submit it to Judge Pro Tem Stephen Zollman for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Stephen Zollman, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT)

Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Admission Of The Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF CARL WESCOTT'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Admission Of The Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions is DENIED. The rules of civil procedure apply equally to parties represented by counsel and those who forgo attorney representation. Rappleyea v. Campbell, 8 Cal. 4th 975, 984-85 (1994); see Robert J. v. Catherine D., 171 Cal. App. 4th 1500, 1527 (2009) (holding "pro per litigants are held to the same standard as parties represented by trained legal counsel." The absence of a Separate Statement by the moving party is fatal to the motion. See Cal. R. Ct. Rule 3.1345(a); Mills v. U.S. Bank, 166 Cal. App. 4th 871, 893 (2008) Here, WESTCOTT failed to file a Separate Statement which contributed to the denial of the motion along with his failure to lodge courtesy copies of the moving papers at least three days before a hearing. S.F. Civ. L.R. 2.7(B). Both parties are admonished to familiarize themselves with the local rules. In addition, the Court finds that WESCOTT did not meet and confer in good faith, instead resorted to facetious banter which diminished whatever merit there was to his position and represents an abuse of the discovery process. Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2023.010(i). Flaunting a procedural requirement of sending a proof of service along with the discovery requests was equally problematic. The request for monetary sanctions by both parties is DENIED.

The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim. The proposed order is to be emailed to Judge Pro Tem Van Zandt per tentative ruling instructions.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter J. Van Zandt; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (DISC302)

Motion To Disqualify Defense Counsel Harold P. Smith
Off Calendar Apr-03-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF CARL WESCOTT Motion To Disqualify Defense Counsel Harold P. Smith, as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to disqualify his adversary's counsel is DENIED. Generally only one who has or had an attorney-client relationship with a lawyer has standing to seek to disqualify him. (Great Lakes Construction, Inc. v. Burman (2010) 186 Cal.App. 4th 1347, 1356.) Moreover, the sole evidence in support of the motion is plaintiff's own declaration, which mentions counsel but once and in passing. Further, a party has a right to counsel of his choice. (Mills Land & Water Co. v. Golden West Refining Co. (1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 116, 126.)

Counsel for defendant is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Mar-14-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-14-2018 Set For Trial On Oct-15-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Continued To Feb-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Require A Bond Pursuant To Ccp 391.1
Off Calendar Jan-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Carl Wescott, T:415.335.5000, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOHN FIELDS' Motion To Require A Bond Pursuant To CCP 391.1 is DENIED. Mr. Fields has failed to satisfy his burden to show that there is not a reasonable probability that plaintiff Carl Wescott will prevail in this case. Mr. Wescott's complaint alleges that Mr. Fields committed various acts of fraud, yet Mr. Fields did not deny that he committed fraud nor did he provide any evidence showing that he didn't commit fraud. Mr. Fields' evidence focuses on the liability of his former company, rather on his own liability. Mr. Fields' "belief" that "there is no basis" for any of Mr. Wescott's claims does not negate the allegations of fraud against him.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.
